title: koeleria littoralis
intro: Koeleria littoralis, also known as tufted hair-grass, is a perennial grass species native to Europe and Asia, characterized by its compact, clump-forming habit and delicate, hair-like inflorescences. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues due to its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Koeleria littoralis include its potential to alleviate symptoms of respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its ability to boost the immune system and reduce inflammation. Koeleria littoralis is commonly prepared for medicinal use through infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be administered orally or topically, depending on the specific application.
